request.description:
### Upload Template Image API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to upload a template image. The uploaded image can be used for design layouts or graphical templates within the system.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/UploadTempleteImage

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- form-data
    

**Parameters:**

- file (File) – Required (Template image file)
    
- IsUserUpload (boolean) – Required
    
    - true → Uploaded by user
        
    - false → System/admin upload
        

**Example (form-data):**

| Key | Type | Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| file | File | Ali Raza Picture.jpg |
| IsUserUpload | Text | true |

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Delete Template Image API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to delete a specific template image by passing the Template Image ID.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- PATCH
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/TempleteImage/{templateImageId}/delete

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/TempleteImage/d2e8cfe4-8651-43e7-ab28-ed2a2e9e9c25/delete

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- templateImageId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- confirmation that the template image has been deleted

### Get Template Image List API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to retrieve the list of uploaded template images.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/GetTempleteImage

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- No query parameters required.
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data (array)

### Get Template Detail API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to retrieve detailed information about a specific template by passing the Template ID.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/GetTemplete/{templateId}

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/GetTemplete/aefac287-dbe8-4222-bbe5-9d20749b7ef5

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- templateId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Templates API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to retrieve the list of templates based on filtering and pagination parameters.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/TempleteS

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- sportId (Guid) – Optional (Filter by sport)
    
- userId (Guid) – Optional (Filter templates created by specific user)
    
- isApproved (boolean) – Optional (Filter approved/unapproved templates)
    
- templeteType (int) – Optional (Template category/type)
    
- pageNumber (int) – Required
    
- pageSize (int) – Required
    
- searchTerm (string) – Optional (Search by template name)
    

**Example:**

```
{  "sportId": "b1a1cfd3-48b9-43e5-a00b-ff248a623f7a",  "userId": "b1a1cfd3-48b9-43e5-a00b-ff248a623f7a",  "isApproved": true,  "templeteType": 1,  "pageNumber": 1,  "pageSize": 10,  "searchTerm": ""}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Add or Update Template API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to create a new template or update an existing one.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/AddOrUpdatedTemplete

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- form-data
    

**Parameters:**

- Title (string) – Required (Template title)
    
- SportId (Guid) – Required
    
- Files (File) – Required (One or multiple template images/files)
    
- TempleteId (Guid) – Optional
    
    - If provided → Update existing template
        
    - If not provided → Create new template
        
- TempleteType (int) – Required (Template category/type)
    
- DocumentTypes (int) – Required (Can pass multiple values)
    

---

### Example (form-data)

| Key | Type | Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Title | Text | Ali Raza |
| SportId | Text | 7aae9461-34c3-46bd-ac24-04da004e... |
| Files | File | (Multiple files) |
| TempleteId | Text | d5e77b6b-3f61-445e-a699-f25b9524... |
| TempleteType | Text | 1 |
| DocumentTypes | Text | 1 |
| DocumentTypes | Text | 2 |

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Bulk Approve Templates API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to approve or reject multiple templates at once. Once a template is approved, it becomes visible in the **Mobile App**.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/BulkApproveTemplates

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- JSON
    

**Parameters:**

- templateIds (array of Guid) – Required
    
    - List of template IDs to approve or reject
        
- isApproved (boolean) – Required
    
    - true → Approve templates (visible in Mobile App)
        
    - false → Reject templates (hidden from Mobile App)
        

---

### Example Request

```
{  "templateIds": [    "a1e5658d-0df2-4b04-b94c-2f3b64a8c110",    "7c4dfc15-75f8-4cc5-8dc8-d42e5c5dc5f1",    "2d24f75b-6b33-4be1-9e4e-8d2b8d134e42"  ],  "isApproved": true}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Delete Template API (Admin – Template Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – Template module** to permanently delete a specific template.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- DELETE
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/DeleteTemplate/{templateId}

 ```

---

### Path Parameter

- templateId (Guid) – Required
    
    - The unique ID of the template to be deleted.
        

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/Templete/DeleteTemplate/aefac287-dbe8-4222-bbe5-9d20749b7ef5

 ```

---

### Request Body

- Not required
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Club Count By Sport API (Admin)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel** to retrieve the total number of clubs grouped by sport. It provides an overview of how many clubs are registered under each sport category in the system.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/ClubOverview/ClubCountBySport

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body or query parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {admin_token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Clubs By Sport API (Admin)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel** to retrieve the list of clubs by passing a specific Sport ID. It supports pagination and search functionality to filter clubs efficiently.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/ClubOverview/Clubs

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- sportId (Guid) – Required
    
- pageNumber (int) – Required
    
- pageSize (int) – Required
    
- searchTerm (string) – Optional
    

**Example:**

```
{  "sportId": "b1a1cfd3-48b9-43e5-a00b-ff248a623f7a",  "pageNumber": 1,  "pageSize": 10,  "searchTerm": ""}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Club Info API (Admin)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel** to retrieve detailed information of a single club by passing the Club ID in the route. It returns complete club profile details for admin review and management.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/ClubOverview/ClubInfo/{clubId}

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/ClubOverview/ClubInfo/9f110284-a38e-423b-91af-750e8f284895

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

**Path Parameter:**

- clubId (Guid) – Required
    

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {admin_token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Player Count By Sport API (Admin)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel** to retrieve the total number of players grouped by sport. It provides a summary view that helps the admin understand how many players are registered under each sport.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/PlayerOverview/PlayerCountBySport

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- No query parameters required.
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data (array) containing:
    
    - sportId
        
    - sportName
        
    - playerCount

### Player Info API (Admin)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel** to retrieve detailed information about a specific player by passing the Player ID.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```

 ```

**Example:**


---

### Request Parameters

- playerId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Users API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is part of the **Admin Panel – User Management module**.  
  
It is used to retrieve the list of registered users with pagination and search functionality.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetUsers

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- pageNumber (int) – Required
    
- pageSize (int) – Required
    
- searchTerm (string) – Optional (Filter users by name, email, etc.)
    

**Example:**

```
{  "pageNumber": 1,  "pageSize": 10,  "SearchTerm": ""}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Admin Roles API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to retrieve the list of available admin roles. It is typically used when assigning roles to users.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetAdminRole

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- No query parameters required.
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Add or Update User API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to create a new admin user or update an existing one.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/AddOrUpdateUser

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- userId (string) – Required (If provided → update user, if new → create user)
    
- fullName (string) – Required
    
- email (string) – Required
    
- roleId (Guid) – Required
    
- password (string) – Required (for new user or password reset)
    
- phoneNumber (string) – Optional
    

**Example:**

```
{  "userId": "122238838484899292",  "fullName": "John Doe",  "email": "john.doe@example.com",  "roleId": "117de5ca-b91e-4550-ab5c-08de2d82cf6f",  "password": "StrongPassword123!",  "phoneNumber": "1234567890"}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Request Parameters

- roleId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Role Info By Role ID API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to retrieve detailed information about a specific admin role by passing the Role ID.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetRoleById/{roleId}

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetRoleById/a07e1bcd-2ab2-4b7e-f0f3-08de2da3ee85

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- roleId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- message (string)
    
- data

### Add or Update Role API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to create a new admin role or update an existing role.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/AddorUpdateRole

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- Id (string) – Required
    
    - If the Id exists → updates the role
        
    - If new Id → creates a new role
        
- Name (string) – Required (Role name)
    
- Description (string) – Optional
    

**Example:**

```
{  "Id": "83883838",  "Name": "account",  "Description": "this role for account"}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Permissions By Role ID API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to retrieve the list of permissions assigned to a specific role by passing the Role ID.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetPermissions/{roleId}

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/GetPermissions/db156913-2406-483e-003f-08de2d8xxxxx

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

- roleId (Guid) – Required (Passed in route)
    

No query parameters required.

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Update Permission API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to update role permissions. It allows the admin to assign or remove permissions for a specific role.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/UpdatePermission

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- roleId (Guid) – Required
    
- roleClaims (array) – Required  
      
    Each claim contains:
    
    - roleId (Guid)
        
    - type (string) – Permission key
        
    - value (string) – Permission value
        
    - selected (boolean) – true = assign, false = remove
        
    - description (string)
        
    - group (string) – Module group (e.g., Users, Roles)
        

**Example:**

```
{  "roleId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",  "roleClaims": [    {      "roleId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",      "type": "Permissions.Users.View",      "value": "Permissions.Users.View",      "selected": true,      "description": "Allows viewing users",      "group": "Users"    },    {      "roleId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",      "type": "Permissions.Users.Create",      "value": "Permissions.Users.Create",      "selected": false,      "description": "Allows creating users",      "group": "Users"    }  ]}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- confirmation of updated permissions

### Get Graphic Designer Users API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to retrieve the list of users who are assigned the **Graphic Designer role**.

**Authorization:**

---

- data

### Reset User Password API (Admin – User Management Module)

This endpoint is used in the **Admin Panel – User Management module** to reset the password of any user by an administrator.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Admin Access)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/admin/UserManagement/ResetUserPassword

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- raw (JSON)
    

**Parameters:**

- UserId (string) – Required
    
- NewPassword (string) – Required
    

**Example:**

```
{  "UserId": "83883838",  "NewPassword": "StrongPassword123!"}

 ```

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- confirmation of password reset

### Get Notifications API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of notifications for the logged-in user. It returns all notifications related to matches, posts, events, or other system activities.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Notification/GetNotifications

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Mark All Notifications as Read API

This endpoint is used to mark all notifications as read for the logged-in user. Once this API is called, all unread notifications will be updated to read status.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Notification/markallread

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Update Notification Status API

This endpoint is used to enable or disable notifications for the authenticated user. It is typically called from the mobile application settings screen when the user turns notifications on or off. The API uses the **POST** method and requires a JSON request body containing the `isNotificationEnabled` flag. A value of `true` enables notifications, while `false` disables them. The request also requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s identity. Once processed successfully, the system updates the user’s notification preference and returns a confirmation response. If the token is invalid or the request data is incorrect, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters (JSON):**

- `isNotificationEnabled` (boolean, required):
    
    - `true` → Enable notifications
        
    - `false` → Disable notifications
        

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the notification status is updated successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or the update request is invalid.

### Update Club Profile API

This endpoint is used to update the profile information of a club account. When the user selects the **Club Role**, this API allows the club to modify its profile details such as club name, sport, role, contact number, address, and club logo/image. The request is sent using the **POST** method with **form-data** because it supports uploading a file (club profile image). A valid **Bearer Token** is required in the Authorization header to ensure that only the authenticated club can update its own profile information.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    
- Body type: form-data
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `file` (File) – Club logo or profile image
    
- `FullName` (Text) – Club name
    
- `sportId` (Text) – Selected sport ID
    
- `roleId` (Text) – Club role ID
    
- `PhoneNumber` (Text) – Club contact number
    
- `Address` (Text) – Club location/address
    

**Response:**

- Returns success message if the club profile is updated successfully
    
- Returns error response if authorization fails or required fields are missing

### Add Club API

This endpoint is used to create a new club. It allows a player to create a club while completing or updating their player profile. The request is sent using the **POST** method with **form-data** because it supports uploading a club logo image. Once successfully created, the club can be selected and linked to the player profile.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    
- Body type: form-data
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `FullName` (Text) – Required. Name of the club.
    
- `SportId` (Text) – Required. The sport ID associated with the club.
    
- `File` (File) – Optional. Club logo or profile image.
    

**Response:**

- Returns success response with created club details (including ClubId).
    
- Returns error response if required fields are missing or authorization fails.

formdata.description: OfficialId don't need to send in case of add

request.description:
### Add Or Update Club Official API

This endpoint is used to create a new club official or update an existing club official profile. It allows a club (authorized user with club role) to manage officials such as coaches, managers, or other staff members associated with the club. The request uses **POST** method with **form-data** because it supports image upload.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    
- Body type: form-data
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `Name` (Text) – Required. Full name of the club official.
    
- `Gender` (Text) – Required. Gender of the official (e.g., 0 = Male, 1 = Female).
    
- `JoiningDate` (Text) – Required. Official joining date.
    
- `DateOfBirth` (Text) – Optional. Date of birth of the official (e.g., 08/18/1980).
    
- `DesignationId` (Text) – Required. Designation ID (e.g., Coach, Manager, etc.).
    
- `ClubId` (Text) – Required. ID of the club.
    
- `File` (File) – Optional. Official profile image.
    
- `OfficialId` (Text) – Optional. Required only when updating an existing official. Not required when creating a new one.
    

**Response:**

- Returns success response with official details after creation or update.
    
- Returns error response if required fields are missing or authorization fails.

### Get Club Profile API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the complete profile details of the logged-in club. It returns all registered information related to the club account and is mainly used when loading the club profile screen inside the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Club/GetClubProfile

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Club Officials By Department API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of club officials grouped by department. It helps the club view all registered staff members such as coaches, managers, medical staff, and other officials based on their assigned department. This API is mainly used in the club management section of the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Club/GetClubOfficialsByDepartment

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Remove Club Official API

This endpoint is used to remove a club official from the club’s official staff list. It allows the club to delete an existing official (such as a coach, manager, or department staff member) by passing the **ClubOfficialId** in the request URL. This API is mainly used in the club management module when the club wants to remove an official from their profile.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- PATCH
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Club/club-officials/{clubOfficialId}/remove

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

**Path Parameter:**

- **clubOfficialId** (Required)
    
    - The unique identifier of the club official that needs to be removed.
        

Example:

```
9c3c76d9-350b-48be-8bf9-e430df58df56

 ```

---

### Headers

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On successful removal, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string) confirming the official has been removed
    
- data (optional)

### Update Player Profile API

This endpoint is used to update the profile information of the authenticated player. The API allows the player to modify personal details such as full name, date of birth, gender, sport, role, playing position, height, weight, strong foot, and club history. It also allows updating the profile image. The API uses the **POST** method with **form-data** because it supports file upload. A valid **Bearer Token** is required in the Authorization header to authenticate the player. If the token is missing or invalid, the request will be rejected.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    
- Body type: form-data
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `file` (File) – Profile image
    
- `FullName` (Text)
    
- `DateOfBirth` (Text)
    
- `Gender` (Text)
    
- `sportId` (Text)
    
- `roleId` (Text)
    
- `PlayingPositionId` (Text)
    
- `WeightKg` (Text)
    
- `HeightCm` (Text)
    
- `StrongFoot` (Text)
    
- `ClubHistory[0].ClubId` (Text)
    
- `ClubHistory[0].StartYear` (Text)
    
- `ClubHistory[0].EndYear` (Text)
    
- `ClubHistory[0].IsCurrentClub` (Text)
    

**Response:**

- Returns success message if profile is updated successfully
    
- Returns error response if validation or authorization fails

request.description:
### Get Player Profile API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the profile details of the authenticated player. It returns the player’s personal information, physical attributes, playing position, strong foot, club history, and uploaded images. The API uses the **GET** method and requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to identify the logged-in player. No request body is required because the system extracts the player information directly from the provided token. If the token is missing or invalid, the API returns an authorization error.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns the complete profile details of the authenticated player
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails

### Edit Player Profile API

This endpoint is used to update an existing player’s profile information. It allows authorized users (such as a club or the player) to modify personal details, physical attributes, playing position, strong foot, profile image, and club history. The API uses the **POST** method and requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s identity and permissions. The request must be sent as **form-data**, including both text fields and an optional profile image file.

The API supports updating club history details such as club ID, start year, end year, and whether it is the current club. Once processed successfully, the system updates the player’s profile and returns a confirmation response. If the token is invalid, expired, or required fields are missing, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters (form-data):**

- `file` (file, optional): Player profile image
    
- `FullName` (text, required)
    
- `DateOfBirth` (text/date, required)
    
- `Gender` (integer, required)
    
- `WeightKg` (integer, optional)
    
- `HeightCm` (integer, optional)
    
- `StrongFoot` (integer, required)
    
- `PlayingPositionId` (string/GUID, required)
    
- `ClubHistory[index].ClubId` (string/GUID, required)
    
- `ClubHistory[index].StartYear` (integer, required)
    
- `ClubHistory[index].EndYear` (integer, optional)
    
- `ClubHistory[index].IsCurrentClub` (boolean, required)
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the player profile is updated successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or validation errors occur.

request.description:
### Get Players By Category API

This endpoint is used to retrieve a list of players based on a specific player category. It is typically used in the mobile application to filter and display players according to selected categories (e.g., position, level, type, or other classification defined in the system). The API uses the **GET** method and requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s identity. Once authorized, the system returns a list of players that belong to the specified category. If the token is invalid or the category parameter is missing or incorrect, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- Category parameter (required – passed as query or route parameter depending on implementation): Defines which player category to filter by
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of players matching the selected category.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or the category is invalid.

### Remove Player API

This endpoint is used to remove or deactivate a specific player from the system. It requires the **PlayerId** to be passed as a route parameter in the URL and must be called with a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s identity and permissions. The API uses the **PATCH** method and does not require a request body. Once authorized and processed successfully, the system removes or deactivates the specified player record and returns a confirmation response. If the token is invalid, expired, or the player ID does not exist, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `playerId` (string/GUID, required – passed in URL): Unique identifier of the player to be removed
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the player is removed successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or the player ID is invalid.

### Get Media Gallery API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the media gallery images of the authenticated user. It allows players to view all uploaded photos or media items associated with their profile inside the mobile application. The API uses the **GET** method and requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to ensure that only the logged-in user can access their gallery. Once authorized, the system returns a list of gallery images along with their related details. If the token is missing, invalid, or expired, the API returns an authorization error.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of media gallery images for the authenticated user.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or no media is available.

### Get Social Stats API (Player Dashboard)

Returns aggregated Likes, Impressions, and Shares per social platform (Facebook, Instagram) across all posts that the authenticated user has published through Fubaza.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
- User is identified via JWT

**Response (success):**

```
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Social-Media-Statistik erfolgreich abgerufen.",
  "Data": {
    "platforms": [
      { "platform": "Facebook",  "totalLikes": 0, "totalImpressions": 0, "totalShares": 0 },
      { "platform": "Instagram", "totalLikes": 0, "totalImpressions": 0, "totalShares": 0 }
    ]
  },
  "Error": []
}
```

**Notes:**

- Values are aggregated from the latest `PostInsightSnapshot` per `PostTarget`
- A Hangfire job refreshes the snapshots once per day at 03:00 UTC (manually triggerable in `/hangfire` dashboard)
- Posts published before the post-id-persistence bug-fix went live cannot be tracked (no external Meta post ID stored)
- Platforms with no data are returned with 0/0/0 so the frontend always sees a stable shape

### Get Sports List API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available sports from the system. It is typically used in the mobile application to populate dropdowns or selection fields where users need to choose a sport. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body. Once called, the system returns a list of sports including their basic details such as ID and name. If the request fails due to server issues or invalid configuration, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of sports with their details.
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails.

StartFragment

### Get Roles List API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available user roles from the system. It is typically used in the mobile application during the registration process to allow users to select their account type, such as **Player** or **Club**. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body. Once called, the system returns a list of available roles with their identifiers and names. If the request fails due to server or configuration issues, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of roles (e.g., Player, Club).
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails.

### Get Playing Positions API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of playing positions based on a specific sport. It requires a **Sport ID** as a route parameter in the URL, and it returns all available positions related to that sport. This API is typically used in the mobile application during player registration or profile setup, where the user selects their sport first and then chooses their playing position accordingly. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `sportId` (GUID/string, required – passed in URL): The unique identifier of the selected sport
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of playing positions associated with the provided sport ID.
    
- Returns an error response if the sport ID is invalid or not found

### Get Strong Foot List API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available strong foot options for players, such as right foot, left foot, or both. It is typically used in the mobile application during the player profile creation or update process, where the user selects their dominant foot. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body. Once called, the system returns a list of strong foot options with their identifiers and names. If the request fails due to server or configuration issues, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of strong foot options (e.g., Right, Left, Both).
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails.

### Get Designations List API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available official designations in the system, such as referee roles or other match-related official positions. It is typically used in the mobile application when creating or managing official profiles, allowing users to select their designation from a predefined list. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body. Once called, the system returns a list of designations with their identifiers and names. If the request fails due to server or configuration issues, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of official designations with their details.
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails.

### Get Events List API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of events associated with a specific sport. It requires a **SportId** as a route parameter in the URL and returns all available events related to that sport. This API is typically used in the mobile application while creating a match summary, allowing users to select the relevant event before generating match details. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `sportId` (string/GUID, required – passed in URL): Unique identifier of the selected sport
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of events associated with the provided sport ID.
    
- Returns an error response if the sport ID is invalid or no events are found.

### Get Template Type API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available template types in the system. It allows the mobile application to load different template categories that can be used when generating posters or social media content. The API uses the **GET** method and does not require a request body. Once called, the system returns a list of template types with their identifiers and names. This API is typically used before fetching templates to filter results based on the selected template type. If the request fails due to server or configuration issues, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Not required (unless specified by system configuration)
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of template types with their details.
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails.

StartFragment

### Remove Background API

This endpoint is used to remove the background from an uploaded image and return the processed image in Base64 format. It is typically used in the mobile application when users upload profile pictures or media that require background removal. The API uses the **POST** method and requires the image file to be sent as **form-data** with the key `image`. Once the image is processed successfully, the system returns the updated image encoded in Base64 format. If the image is invalid, missing, or processing fails, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters:**

- `image` (file, required): Image file to process (sent as form-data)
    

**Response:**

- Returns the processed image in Base64 format after background removal.
    
- Returns an error response if the image upload or processing fails.
    

EndFragment

### Remove Background API

This endpoint is used to remove the background from an uploaded image and return the processed image in Base64 format. It is typically used in the mobile application when users upload profile pictures or media that require background removal. The API uses the **POST** method and requires the image file to be sent as **form-data** with the key `image`. Once the image is processed successfully, the system returns the updated image encoded in Base64 format. If the image is invalid, missing, or processing fails, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters:**

- `image` (file, required): Image file to process (sent as form-data)
    

**Response:**

- Returns the processed image in Base64 format after background removal.
    
- Returns an error response if the image upload or processing fails.
    

EndFragment

### Generate Poster API

This endpoint is used to generate a customized match or event poster with caption details and uploaded images. It allows users to create social media-ready posts by providing match information such as title, subtitle, scores, and related images. The API uses the **POST** method and requires data to be sent as **form-data**, including both text fields and image files. Once the request is processed successfully, the system generates the poster and returns the final image encoded in **Base64 format**, which can be directly displayed in the mobile application or uploaded to social media platforms. If any required field is missing or image processing fails, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters (form-data):**

- `Title` (text, required): Main heading of the post
    
- `Subtitle` (text, required): Secondary text (e.g., match stage)
    
- `Score1` (text, required): First team/player score
    
- `Score2` (text, required): Second team/player score
    
- `PlayerImage` (file, required): Player image
    
- `ClubLogo` (file, required): Club logo image
    
- `OpponentLogo` (file, required): Opponent logo image
    
- `Logos[0].logo` (file, optional): Additional sponsor or brand logos
    

**Response:**

- Returns the generated poster image in **Base64 format**.
    
- Returns an error response if generation fails or required data is missing.

### Generate Post Caption API

This endpoint is used to generate a sports-related post caption based on a provided prompt. It allows users to automatically create engaging and ready-to-publish captions for matches, tournaments, or sports events. The API uses the **POST** method and requires a JSON request body containing a `Prompt` field. The system processes the given text and generates a structured, creative caption suitable for social media posting. If the prompt is missing or invalid, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters (JSON):**

- `Prompt` (string, required): Input text describing the sports event or context for generating the caption
    

**Response:**

- Returns the generated sports caption text.
    
- Returns an error response if the prompt is invalid or generation fails.

### Get Templates API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of available post or poster templates based on a selected sport. It allows the mobile application to load different template designs that can be used when generating social media content. The API uses the **POST** method and requires the **SportId** as a mandatory parameter, while **TemplateType** is optional and can be used to filter templates by category or type. The request is sent as **form-data**, and the system returns a list of templates related to the provided sport. If the sport ID is invalid or no templates are available, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters (form-data):**

- `SportId` (string/GUID, required): Unique identifier of the selected sport
    
- `TemplateType` (integer/text, optional): Type or category of templates to filter results
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of templates associated with the given sport ID and template type.
    
- Returns an error response if the request fails or no templates are found.

request.description:
### AI Image Enhancement API

This endpoint is used to enhance or modify images using AI processing. It allows users to upload a template image and optionally additional images, along with a descriptive prompt explaining the desired enhancement or modification. The API uses the **POST** method and requires data to be sent as **form-data**. The `Template` image and `Prompt` text are mandatory, while additional `Images` can be provided optionally to assist in the enhancement process. Once processed, the system applies AI-based modifications such as replacing elements, improving quality, or adapting the image according to the prompt, and returns the enhanced image in the response. If required inputs are missing or processing fails, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Request Parameters (form-data):**

- `Template` (file, required): Base template image to be enhanced
    
- `Prompt` (text, required): Description of the requested modification
    
- `Images` (file, optional): Additional reference images
    

**Response:**

- Returns the AI-enhanced image (typically in Base64 or image format).
    
- Returns an error response if enhancement fails or required data is missing.

### Verify Email API

This endpoint is used to verify the user’s email address by validating the OTP code sent to their email. The user must provide the received **code** in the request body, along with a valid authentication token if required. Once the OTP is confirmed as correct, the system marks the user’s email as verified and allows full access to the account features. If the code is incorrect, expired, or the token is invalid, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required if applicable): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- `code` (string, required): OTP code sent to the user’s email
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the email is verified successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the OTP is invalid, expired, or authorization fails.

request.description:
### SignIn API

This endpoint is used to authenticate an existing user in the system. It requires the user’s **email** and **password** to verify the account credentials, while the **fcmToken** parameter is optional and can be used to update the device token for push notifications. The request must be sent in JSON format. If the credentials are valid, the API returns a success response along with the user details and an authentication token for authorized access to the system. If the email or password is incorrect, the API returns an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `email` (string, required): Registered user email address
    
- `password` (string, required): User account password
    
- `fcmToken` (string, optional): Firebase token for notifications
    

**Response:**

- Returns user information and authentication token upon successful login.
    
- Returns an error response if authentication fails due to invalid credentials.

### Resend OTP API

This endpoint is used to resend the One-Time Password (OTP) to the user’s registered email address. It is typically called when the user does not receive the OTP or requests a new verification code during account verification or password reset. The request requires only the user’s **email** in the request body. Once processed, the system generates and sends a new OTP to the provided email address. If the email is not registered or the request is invalid, the API returns an appropriate error response.

**Request Parameters:**

- `email` (string, required): User’s registered email address
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the OTP is resent successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the email is invalid or the OTP cannot be sent.

### Reset Password API

This endpoint is used to reset the user’s account password after successful OTP verification. It allows the user to set a new password by providing the registered **email** address along with the **newPassword** in the request body. This API is typically called after the OTP has been verified to complete the password recovery process. Once the request is validated, the system updates the user’s password and returns a success response. If the email is not found, the OTP process was not completed, or the request data is invalid, the API returns an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `email` (string, required): Registered user email address
    
- `newPassword` (string, required): New password to be set
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the password is reset successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the reset process fails or the request is invalid.

console.warn("Refresh token not found in response");

request.description:
This endpoint is used to register a new user in the system. The request requires the user’s **email** and **password** to create an account, while the **fcmToken** parameter is optional and can be used to store the device token for push notifications. The API accepts a JSON body containing these parameters. Upon successful registration, the API returns a response indicating success along with the newly created user details and an authentication token (if applicable). In case the email already exists or the request data is invalid, the API responds with an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `email` (string, required): User email address
    
- `password` (string, required): User account password
    
- `fcmToken` (string, optional): Firebase token for notifications
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message with user information when registration is completed successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if registration fails due to invalid input or duplicate email.

### Verify OTP API

This endpoint is used to verify the One-Time Password (OTP) sent to the user’s email address for account verification or password reset purposes. The user must provide the registered **email** and the received **code** (OTP) in the request body. The API validates the OTP against the stored verification code and confirms whether it is correct and still valid. If the verification is successful, the system allows the user to proceed with the next step, such as activating the account or resetting the password. If the OTP is incorrect or expired, the API returns an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `email` (string, required): User’s registered email address
    
- `code` (string, required): OTP code sent to the email
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the OTP is verified successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the OTP is invalid, expired, or incorrect.

exec: // Check if refreshToken exists and save it to an environment variable

console.warn("Refresh token not found in response");

request.description:
### Social Login API

This endpoint is used to authenticate users through social login providers such as **Google**, **Facebook**, or **Apple**. Instead of using email and password, the user signs in using a valid social access token generated by the selected provider. The request includes the provider name, the authentication token, and an optional **fcmToken** for enabling push notifications on the user’s device. Once the token is verified successfully, the system logs in the user (or creates an account if the user does not already exist) and returns a success response with user details and an authentication token. If the token is invalid or the provider is not supported, the API responds with an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `provider` (string, required): Social platform name (e.g., "google", "facebook", "apple")
    
- `token` (string, required): Access token received from the social provider
    
- `fcmToken` (string, optional): Firebase token for push notifications
    

**Response:**

- Returns user information and authentication token upon successful social login.
    
- Returns an error response if authentication fails or the token is invalid.

### Change Password API

This endpoint is used to update the user’s account password after successful OTP verification during the password reset process. It allows the user to set a new password by providing the **NewPassword** field in the request body. The API is typically called once the system confirms the user’s identity through OTP, ensuring that only authorized users can change their password. Upon successful execution, the API returns a confirmation message indicating that the password has been updated. If the request data is invalid or the reset session is not authorized, the API responds with an appropriate error message.

**Request Parameters:**

- `NewPassword` (string, required): The new password to be set for the user account
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the password is changed successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the password update fails or the request is invalid.

### Get User Pages API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the authenticated user’s connected social media pages, such as Facebook and Instagram pages. It is mainly used in the mobile application when the user wants to schedule a post and needs to select which social page the content should be published on. The API requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s account and fetch the associated social pages. Once authorized, the system returns a list of available pages linked to the user. If the token is invalid or the user has no connected pages, an appropriate error response is returned.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body is required
    

**Response:**

- Returns a list of connected Facebook and Instagram pages for the user.
    
- Returns an error response if authorization fails or no pages are available.

### Update Language API

This endpoint is used to update the preferred language of the authenticated user in the system. It is typically called when a user changes the application language from their profile or settings section. The API requires a valid **Bearer Token** in the Authorization header to verify the user’s identity. Once the token is validated, the system updates the user’s language preference and returns a success response. If the token is missing, invalid, or expired, the API returns an authorization error.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- Language (string, required): Selected language code (e.g., "en", "de")
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the language is updated successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the token is invalid or the update request fails.

### Delete Account API

This endpoint is used to permanently delete the authenticated user account from the system. It is typically called from the user profile section when a user chooses to remove their account. The API requires authorization, meaning the request must include a valid **Bearer Token** in the request headers to confirm the user’s identity. Once the token is verified, the system deletes the associated account and returns a success response. If the token is missing, invalid, or expired, the API responds with an authorization error.

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (required): User authentication token
    

**Request Parameters:**

- No request body is required
    
- Token must be passed in the Authorization header
    

**Response:**

- Returns a success message when the account is deleted successfully.
    
- Returns an error response if the token is invalid or the deletion request fails.

### Get Calendar Events API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of calendar events. It returns both **match events** and **post events** in a single response. This API is typically used to display events in a calendar view inside the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetCalendarEvents

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data
    
- For matchday-type items, the data includes `venue` (int): 0 = Home, 1 = Away, 2 = Neutral. Posts have `venue: null`.

### Get Upcoming Matches API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of upcoming matches. It provides details about scheduled matches that have not yet started. This API is mainly used to display upcoming events in the mobile application dashboard or event section.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetUpcomingMatches

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data
    
- The data items now include `venue` (int): 0 = Home, 1 = Away, 2 = Neutral.

### Get Upcoming Posts API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of upcoming posts. It returns posts that are scheduled to be published at a future date and time. This API is typically used by clubs or authorized users to view and manage scheduled posts.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetUpcomingPosts

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Draft Posts API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of draft posts. It returns all posts that are saved as drafts and not yet published. This API is typically used by clubs or authorized users to manage and review their unpublished posts.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetDraftPosts

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Match History API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the list of match history. It returns previously played matches along with their basic details such as teams, scores, dates, and match status. This API is typically used to display past matches in the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetMatchHistory

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data
    
- The data items now include `venue` (int): 0 = Home, 1 = Away, 2 = Neutral.

### Get Match Summary API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the summary details of a specific match. It returns key information about the match such as score, events, statistics, and overall match details. The match ID must be passed in the route.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetMatchSummary/{matchId}

 ```

**Example:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetMatchSummary/37E3EE2F-D20C-4811-99A3-5FA24C300A1E

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

**Path Parameter:**

- matchId (Guid) – Required
    

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Last Match Line Up API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the lineup of the most recent match. It returns the list of players who participated in the last played match, including starting players and substitutes. This API is mainly used to display the previous match lineup in the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetLastMatchLineUp

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

No body parameters are required.

**Headers:**

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Get Match Fairness API

This endpoint is used to retrieve the fairness details of a specific match. It returns match fairness data based on the provided **MatchId**. This API is mainly used to evaluate or display fairness metrics, ratings, or related match integrity information in the mobile application.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- GET
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/GetMatchFairness/{matchId}

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

**Path Parameter:**

- **matchId** (Required)
    
    - The unique identifier of the match.

formdata.description: when using Facebooklogin send true for all pages (Facebook,Instagram). when using InstagramLogin send false.

request.description:
### Schedule Post API

This endpoint is used to schedule a post for future publishing. The club can create or update a post and set a specific date and time for it to be automatically published.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Club Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/SchedulePost

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- form-data
    

**Parameters:**

- Caption (string) – Required
    
- ScheduleDateTime (DateTime – ISO 8601 format) – Required
    
- PostId (Guid) – Optional (Required if updating existing scheduled post)
    
- File (image/video file) – Optional
    
- IsDraft (boolean) – Required (true = save as draft, false = schedule)
    
- PostTarget\[0\].PageId (string) – Required
    
- PostTarget\[0\].AccessToken (string) – Required
    
- PostTarget\[0\].InstagramBusinessId (string) – Required
    

**Example:**

- Caption: I love Pakistan
    
- ScheduleDateTime: 2025-10-13T17:57:00Z
    
- IsDraft: false
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

formdata.description: send same Id of that row for editing and if new then dont add.

request.description:
### Set Match Day API

This endpoint is used to create a new match (set a match day). It allows the system to schedule a match between two clubs by providing match details such as date, organizer club, opponent club, referees, and location.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Authorized Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/SetMatchDay

 ```

---

### Request Body

**Content Type:**

- form-data
    

**Parameters:**

- MatchdayNumber (string/int) – Required
    
- MatchDayDateTime (DateTime – ISO format) – Required
    
- OrganizerClubId (Guid) – Required
    
- OpponentClubId (Guid) – Required
    
- Referee (string) – Required
    
- AssistantReferee1 (string) – Optional
    
- AssistantReferee2 (string) – Optional
    
- Location (string) – Required
    
- Venue (int) – Optional (0 = Home default, 1 = Away, 2 = Neutral)
    

**Example:**

- MatchdayNumber: 1
    
- MatchDayDateTime: 2025-10-05T15:35:00.000
    
- OrganizerClubId: C074E3B3-303D-4AF8-8F7B-5F65FF841F71
    
- OpponentClubId: ce07033e-dfbe-4aaf-89f5-029619e2aa1a
    
- Referee: John Smith
    
- AssistantReferee1: Alex Brown
    
- AssistantReferee2: David Lee
    
- Location: Spain
    
- Venue: 0
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data

### Start Match API

This endpoint is used to start a match. The club (or authorized user) triggers this API when the match officially begins. It records the match start time in the system.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required – Club Role)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/StartMatch

 ```

---

### Request Parameters

**Query Parameters:**

- MatchId (Guid) – Required
    
- MatchStartDateTime (DateTime – ISO 8601 format) – Required
    

**Example:**

- MatchId: 9145ab6e-4499-4c76-868b-43ba56a814fc
    
- MatchStartDateTime: 2025-09-20T06:58:54Z
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string)
    
- data (object)

formdata.description: send same Id of that row for editing and if new then dont add.

Assist provider (Vorlagengeber). Optional. Only allowed for Goal events. Must belong to the same team as the scorer (ClubId) and (when a match lineup exists) be part of the squad. Cannot be the same as PlayerId.

request.description:
### Set Match Line Up API

This endpoint is used to set or update the lineup for a specific match. It allows the authorized user (such as club admin or event manager) to assign players to a match by providing the **MatchDayId** and a list of **PlayerIds**. This API is mainly used before the match starts to define the starting lineup and participating players.

---

**Authorization:**

- Bearer Token (Required)
    

**Request Type:**

- POST
    

**Endpoint:**

```
{{base_url}}/api/Event/SetMatchLineUp

 ```

---

### Request Parameters (form-data)

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MatchdayId | Text | Yes | The unique identifier of the match day |
| PlayerIds\[0\] | Text | Yes | Player ID |
| PlayerIds\[1\] | Text | Yes | Player ID |
| PlayerIds\[2\] | Text | Yes | Player ID |
| ... | Text | Yes | Additional Player IDs |

> 

Note: Multiple `PlayerIds` can be passed as an array using index format (PlayerIds\[0\], PlayerIds\[1\], etc.).

 
  

---

### Headers

- Authorization: Bearer {token}
    

---

### Response

On success, the API returns:

- success (boolean)
    
- message (string) confirming lineup has been set
    
- data (optional)
